originalTitle: Zelenskyy fires Ukraine’s army chief and offers Fedorov ‘prominent position’
summary: After nationwide protests Oleksandr Syrskyi is to be replaced in reshuffle and popular defence minister returns to government
content: Volodymyr Zelenskyy has fired Ukraine’s commander-in-chief Oleksandr Syrskyi and offered a “prominent position” to the defence minister he sacked last week in a move that sparked nationwide protests. In a late-night TV address, Zelenskyy said he was appointing Mykhailo Drapatyi, a popular and innovative general, as the new head of Ukraine’s armed forces. Drapatyi would oversee a reorganisation of the army’s command structure, Ukraine’s president said. Zelenskyy said he was bringing Mykhailo Fedorov back into government after his removal as defence minister prompted thousands of people to demonstrate in Kyiv and other Ukrainian cities, in one of the biggest political crises since Russia’s 2022 invasion. Ukraine’s president paid tribute to Syrskyi and credited him with leading the defence of Kyiv and launching a counter-invasion of Russia’s Kursk province in summer 2024, in which foreign tanks rolled on to Russian territory for the first time since the second world war. “We have come a long way. The defence of Ukraine continues. And every soldier deserves to be treated with respect,” Zelenskyy said, adding that he was grateful to Syrskyi and to “every one of our soldiers” fighting on the frontline. Zelenskyy will be hoping that his radical reshuffle of the army’s leadership will assuage protesters who have taken to the streets demanding Syrskyi’s removal. They had given a deadline of Friday for Fedorov to be reinstated and promised “indefinite protests” if their request was ignored. Fedorov welcomed Drapatyi’s appointment. “This brings a breath of fresh air and new hope to the struggle of free people for freedom and justice. It is a voice for change that could not be ignored,” he said. He added: “The high expectations of the Ukrainian people must be met with a clear vision for ending the war for independence, a strong team and decisive actions aimed at saving the lives of our soldiers, automating the frontline, delivering asymmetric strikes and crippling the Russian economy.” Drapatyi, the new commander-in-chief, is well-regarded within Ukraine’s army and is liked by the public. He is viewed as an ally of Fedorov, whose attempts to reform the military and force it to adopt modern technology and data led to a bitter clash with the old-school Syrskyi. A major general, Drapatyi is commander of Ukraine’s joint forces group. He has been fighting Russia since its covert attempt in 2014 to take over the eastern Donbas region. He became famous nationally when he drove an infantry fighting vehicle over a barricade set up by pro-Russian militants in the city of Mariupol. The Ukrainian media outlet Babel described Drapatyi as one of Ukraine’s most capable battlefield commanders. It said his new job would be fundamentally about reforming a large institution rather than commanding troops in combat. Zelenskyy said he and Drapatyi agreed that Ukraine’s long-range drone attacks on targets in Russia such as oil refineries would continue. “Our programme of precision strikes will be implemented with absolute precision,” he said, promising a smooth and coordinated handover following Syrskyi’s departure. He also said the new commander-in-chief would be tasked with updating the country’s defence strategy and reforming its structure. Another pressing issue is mobilisation, at a time when after nearly five years of full-scale war few Ukrainians are ready to volunteer. It is unclear what Fedorov’s new role will be, amid reports that he was insisting on being given his old job back as defence minister. This has now been filled by Yevhen Khmara, a former special operations commander. Zelenskyy said he met Fedorov on Tuesday. “I offered Mykhailo a prominent position within the government that would enable him to unify the technological sector of our state and ensure its development,” he said, without giving further details. Zelenskyy added: “Our shared wish is one – victory over the enemy and conditions at the front and in pressure on Russia that would force Russia toward peace.” He said “a significant path has been travelled, the defence of Ukraine continues, and every warrior deserves dignified treatment”.
chineseTitle: 泽连斯基解雇乌克兰陆军参谋长并为费多罗夫提供“显要职位”
chineseSummary: 经过全国范围的抗议后，亚历山大·西尔斯基（Oleksandr Syrskyi）将在改组中被替换，受欢迎的国防部长重返政府
chineseContent: 弗拉基米尔·泽伦斯基(Volodymyr Zelenskyy)解雇了乌克兰总司令亚历山大·西尔斯基(Oleksandr Syrskyi)，并向他上周解职的国防部长提供了“重要职位”，此举引发了全国范围的抗议。泽连斯基在深夜电视讲话中表示，他将任命米哈伊洛·德拉帕蒂（Mykhailo Drapatii）这位颇受欢迎且富有创新精神的将军为乌克兰武装部队新任领导人。乌克兰总统表示，德拉帕蒂将监督军队指挥结构的重组。泽连斯基表示，在米哈伊洛·费多罗夫被免去国防部长职务后，他将让他重返政府，导致数千人在基辅和其他乌克兰城市举行示威，这是自俄罗斯 2022 年入侵以来最大的政治危机之一。乌克兰总统向西尔斯基表示敬意，并称赞他领导了基辅保卫战，并于2024年夏天对俄罗斯库尔斯克省发动了反入侵，这是二战以来外国坦克首次驶入俄罗斯领土。泽伦斯基说：“我们已经走了很长一段路。乌克兰的保卫工作仍在继续。每一位士兵都应该受到尊重。”他补充说，他感谢西尔斯基和在前线战斗的“我们的每一位士兵”。泽伦斯基希望他对军队领导层的彻底改组能够安抚走上街头要求下台的抗议者。他们给了费多罗夫复职的最后期限，并承诺如果他们的要求被忽视，他们将“无限期抗议”。费多罗夫对德拉帕蒂的任命表示欢迎。 “这给自由人民争取自由和正义的斗争带来了新鲜空气和新希望。这是一个不容忽视的变革声音，”他说。他补充说：“必须通过明确的愿景来满足乌克兰人民的高期望。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
